title: rosa canina var. frutetorum
intro: Rosa canina var. frutetorum, also known as the dwarf dog rose, is a shrub-like plant that belongs to the rose family (Rosaceae) and is native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Rosa canina var. frutetorum include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. The plant can be prepared as a herbal remedy through infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often used to make rosehip tea, oil, and capsules.
